content:
When using Clang 14 with Bindgen (at least in Arch Linux) did you noticed that the write field from file_operations is using a mut* instead of a const*? Currently I have

// snip
#[repr(C)]
#[derive(Copy, Clone)]
pub struct file_operations {
    pub write: ::core::option::Option<
        unsafe extern "C" fn(
            arg1: *mut file,
            arg2: *mut c_types::c_char,
            arg3: usize,
            arg4: *mut loff_t,
        ) -> isize,
    >,
// more code

But instead we need

    pub write: ::core::option::Option<
        unsafe extern "C" fn(
            arg1: *mut file,
            arg2: *const c_types::c_char,
            arg3: usize,
            arg4: *mut loff_t,
        ) -> isize,
    >

And to make this work I needed to do some sed sorcery to change it into the same thing

--- a/rust/Makefile
+++ b/rust/Makefile
@@ -304,6 +304,8 @@ quiet_cmd_bindgen = BINDGEN $@

 $(obj)/bindings_generated.rs: private bindgen_target_flags = \
     $(shell grep -v '^\#\|^$$' $(srctree)/$(src)/bindgen_parameters)
+$(obj)/bindings_generated.rs: private bindgen_target_extra = ; \
+    sed -Ei '/struct file_operations/,/arg2: \*/ s|(arg2: \*.*)|\1\n//m|' $@; sed -Ei '/^\/\/m/,/arg2/ s|(arg2: \*)mut|\1const|' $@
 $(obj)/bindings_generated.rs: $(src)/kernel/bindings_helper.h \
     $(src)/bindgen_parameters FORCE
    $(call if_changed_dep,bindgen)

Did this happen to someone?
I can't reproduce it with Clang 14.0.1. Which is your exact Clang (in particular, libclang) and bindgen versions?
I see Arch Linux has Clang 14.0.6 (not sure if patched)
I was using Clang 14.0.6 and Bindgen 0.56.0, although I've reproduced the issue in 0.60.1. And by the way, what's the patch you've mentioned and which distro are you on?
I tried with Clang 14.0.6 compiled from source, and I see the correct result (arg2: *const c_types::c_char,).
By "patched" I meant that I don't know whether Arch Linux uses the vanilla upstream sources or whether they might apply patches on top.

I don't see in the struct definition anything that suggests the const is gated by a kernel config either, at least in v5.19-rc7: https://elixir.bootlin.com/linux/v5.19-rc7/source/include/linux/fs.h#L1968
But, just in case, could you try to reproduce it with one of the .github/workflow configs for instance?
Also, which kernel tree are you using? The rust branch based on rc6, or rust-next, or...?
Finally, could you please post what is the output of bindgen scripts/rust-is-available-bindgen-libclang.h?

Ah, and one more thing since you compiled a newer bindgen: could you please try bindgen --version --verbose? Since 0.60.0 it prints the libclang version being picked up (Emilio added it for us https://github.com/rust-lang/rust-bindgen/issues/2138).

Miguel Ojeda said:

By "patched" I meant that I don't know whether Arch Linux uses the vanilla upstream sources or whether they might apply patches on top.

Arch only uses two patches (one for default strong ssp and another that fixes clang-tidy) but AFAICT there's nothing out of place.

Miguel Ojeda said:

But, just in case, could you try to reproduce it with one of the .github/workflow configs for instance?

With kernel-x86_64-release.config it doesn't fail to compile when I run make rust LLVM=1, but with my .config it seems that I have no luck :oh_no:

Miguel Ojeda said:

Also, which kernel tree are you using? The rust branch based on rc6, or rust-next, or...?

Both v5.18.12 with the 7th version of the patches and with rust from Rust-for-Linux/linux at HEAD (the current commit).

Miguel Ojeda said:

Finally, could you please post what is the output of bindgen scripts/rust-is-available-bindgen-libclang.h?

$ bindgen scripts/rust-is-available-bindgen-libclang.h
scripts/rust-is-available-bindgen-libclang.h:2:9: warning: clang version 14.0.6
  [-W#pragma-messages], err: false
/* automatically generated by rust-bindgen 0.56.0 */

Miguel Ojeda said:

Ah, and one more thing since you compiled a newer bindgen: could you please try bindgen --version --verbose? Since 0.60.0 it prints the libclang version being picked up (Emilio added it for us https://github.com/rust-lang/rust-bindgen/issues/2138).

$ bindgen --version --verbose
bindgen 0.56.0

$ bindgen --version --verbose
bindgen 0.60.1
Clang: clang version 14.0.6
Also here's my .config for the 5.18.12 kernel, though make menuconfig can update it easily. config

Opened up Rust-for-Linux/linux#835 for reviews. Happens that when I was doing a BTF with pahole build it expanded a definition, namely BTF_TYPE_TAG(value), to be __attribute__((btf_type_tag(#value))), this confounds bindgen and makes it discard the const qualifier in attributed function parameters. That's why write got a mut* in the bindings.
Thanks for taking a look at that!
Martin Rodriguez Reboredo has marked this topic as resolved.
